summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the first Sunday of 2025 by welcoming the congregation and connecting the previous week's message on finishing strong to the new year's theme of starting with strength. Using the vivid analogy of drag racing 'funny cars' that explode off the starting line with immense power, he declared 2025 as a year of 'all gas, no brakes.' This means launching into God's purposes with explosive faith, discipline, and dedication, rather than getting stuck in hesitation, fear, or doubt. He emphasized the importance of the church's 21-day fast as a spiritual discipline to draw closer to God, prioritize Him above all else, and seek breakthroughs, guidance, and deliverance. Pastor Travis outlined three key themes for the year: taking spiritual ground by moving forward in maturity and courage; experiencing supernatural increase through faithful investment of time, talents, and treasures; and witnessing God's redemption in every area of life, including time, past failures, relationships, pain, and sin. He passionately shared the vision for the 'Fire Is For You: Books for the Broken' campaign, a practical outworking of the year of increase and investment, aiming to distribute books of hope to hospitals, fire stations, and recovery centers. The message concluded with a call to grow up spiritually, serve actively, and live with a winning mentality, anchored in the ultimate redemption found in Jesus Christ.
mainPoints: 2025 is to be a year of 'all gas, no brakes,' launching into God's purposes with explosive faith and discipline from the very start.
Spiritual growth and supernatural increase are directly tied to personal investment through fasting, serving, and giving.
God is a redeemer who can restore, renew, and bring purpose from our past failures, pain, and brokenness.

versesMentioned.text: Do you not know that in a race all the runners run, but only one gets the prize? Run in such a way as to get the prize.
Have I not commanded you? Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid; do not be discouraged, for the Lord your God will be with you wherever you go.
I will give you every place where you set your foot, as I promised Moses.
When I was a child, I talked like a child, I thought like a child, I reasoned like a child. When I became a man, I put the ways of childhood behind me.
Give, and it will be given to you. A good measure, pressed down, shaken together and running over, will be poured into your lap. For with the measure you use, it will be measured to you.
One person gives freely, yet gains even more; another withholds unduly, but comes to poverty. A generous person will prosper; whoever refreshes others will be refreshed.
But now, this is what the Lord says— he who created you, Jacob, he who formed you, Israel: 'Do not fear, for I have redeemed you; I have summoned you by name; you are mine.'
I will repay you for the years the locusts have eaten— the great locust and the young locust, the other locusts and the locust swarm— my great army that I sent among you.
'For I know the plans I have for you,' declares the Lord, 'plans to prosper you and not to harm you, plans to give you hope and a future.'
For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life.

Monday.scripture: Joshua 1:9 (NIV) - 'Have I not commanded you? Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid; do not be discouraged, for the Lord your God will be with you wherever you go.'
Monday.action: Identify one area where you've been hesitant or afraid to move forward. Take one concrete, courageous step in that area today.
Monday.prayer: Lord, fill me with Your strength and courage. Help me to trust Your presence and take the first step You have for me this year.

Tuesday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 13:11 (NIV) - 'When I became a man, I put the ways of childhood behind me.'
Tuesday.action: Reflect on one 'childish' habit in your speech, attitude, or reaction. Intentionally choose a more mature, Christ-like response in a situation today.
Tuesday.prayer: Father, show me where I need to grow up in my faith. Help me to put away childish ways and live as the man/woman of God You've called me to be.

Wednesday.scripture: Luke 6:38 (NIV) - 'Give, and it will be given to you...'
Wednesday.action: Make a specific investment today. This could be giving an encouraging word, serving someone practically, or giving a financial gift.
Wednesday.prayer: God, I trust that as I sow generously, You will bring increase. Show me how to invest my time, talents, and treasures for Your kingdom today.

Thursday.scripture: Isaiah 43:1 (NIV) - 'Do not fear, for I have redeemed you; I have summoned you by name; you are mine.'
Thursday.action: Write down one past mistake, failure, or pain you are still carrying. In prayer, consciously give it to God, accepting His redemption over that area.
Thursday.prayer: Redeemer God, thank You that You call me by name and I am Yours. I release [name the area] to You. Please restore and use it for Your glory.

Friday.scripture: Proverbs 11:25 (NIV) - 'A generous person will prosper; whoever refreshes others will be refreshed.'
Friday.action: Intentionally seek to refresh one person today. This could be through a phone call, a note, a prayer, or a simple act of kindness.
Friday.prayer: Holy Spirit, lead me to someone who needs refreshment today. Use me as Your vessel to bring hope and encouragement, trusting that You will refresh me in the process.

Saturday.scripture: John 3:16 (NIV) - 'For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son...'
Saturday.action: Share the hope of Christ's redemption with someone. This could be by sharing your testimony, inviting someone to church, or simply expressing God's love.
Saturday.prayer: Jesus, thank You for paying the ultimate price for my redemption. Give me boldness and love to share this life-changing hope with someone who needs to hear it.
